steps.title: Identify the structures around the blank
steps.content:
Before the blank: an independent clause describing manuscripts surviving to the present
After the blank: a gerund phrase (“being passed down...”) that needs to show how they survived

steps.title: Apply the rule for prepositional phrases
steps.content:
To show manner or means, use a prepositional phrase with “by” + gerund: “by being passed down.” A prepositional phrase modifying a clause connects directly without intervening punctuation. Choice B correctly creates “day by being passed down” without punctuation. Choices A and D create false sentence boundaries (period and semicolon). Choice C awkwardly places a comma after the preposition “by.”

That matches choice B — B is correct.
